instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Ways to Renew your ULIP policy
When you invest in a ULIP plan, you have the option to choose your premium payment mode. You can choose to pay your premium as a single lump sum or make periodic payments. Depending on the premium payment frequency, which can be monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, or annually, you are required to pay the premium monthly or according to the chosen frequency. Such payment is known as a renewal premium, since it contributes towards the renewal of your ULIP plan. Paying your premium on time is essential if you wish to continue enjoying your ULIP benefits. Fortunately, there are many ways to make the premium payment needed to renew your ULIP. Read on to discover more about why ULIP renewal is necessary and how you can pay your renewal premium.
The Importance of Renewing Your ULIP Policy
Renewing your ULIP policy is a step that keeps your plan active. Whether you pay monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annually, each renewal maintains the continuity of your policy. Let’s understand why staying consistent with ULIP renewal matters and how it impacts your financial planning.  1
Ensures Continuation of ULIP Benefits
ULIPs offer you a protective life cover that comes in handy in the event of an unfortunate incident. In addition to this, ULIPs also come with an investment component that helps with capital appreciation. By renewing your ULIP policy, you can continue to enjoy these benefits and maintain your financial safety net.
ULIP renewal helps you keep your Life Goals on track
Failing to pay your renewal premium can result in the discontinuance of your policy. That leaves you without an investment to rely on and an insurance cover to take refuge in.
Continue to Enjoy ULIP Tax Benefits
Renewal not only ensures that you continue to reap the benefits of a ULIP, but it also provides tax advantages. As per section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, premiums you pay every year are deductible from your total income for that year. This reduces your taxable income, and by consequence, your tax liability. ULIP tax benefits are subject to changes in tax laws, if any.
What are the different ways to renew your ULIP?
You can pay the premium needed for ULIP renewal either online or offline. There are several options available for making this payment, and depending on the payment options provided by the Insurer, you can choose the option that’s most convenient for you. Here are some of the most common ways to renew your ULIP.
Renewal through cheque payment
This is the most conventional method for renewing ULIPs offline. You can either drop a cheque for the amount of your renewal premium in a designated drop box at your life insurance provider’s branch, or you could submit your cheque at the insurer’s office. Alternatively, you could also submit the cheque at authorised bank partner collection centres.
Renewal through ATM payments
This is another convenient mode of offline ULIP renewal. Many insurers are also allowing policyholders to pay their renewal premiums through bank ATMs. If your insurer has a tie-up with a bank, you can walk into any ATM supported by that banking partner and make your renewal payment.
Renewal through your bank’s auto-debit facility
To ensure that you never miss another premium payment, you can opt for this mode of ULIP renewal. You can activate the auto-debit facility either on your bank account or a Credit card. Each time your premium is due, the amount will be automatically debited from your bank account/credit card.
Renewal through online payment
ULIP renewal can be completed easily through your insurer’s official website, customer portal, or mobile application. You can log in using your registered credentials, select your policy, click on the ‘Renew Policy’ or ‘Pay Premium’ option, and proceed to pay the renewal premium. 2
Most insurers accept multiple payment options, including
Debit and Credit Cards
Bank Transfers (NEFT, RTGS, or IMPS)
UPI
QR Payments
Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL)
Payment links
After successful payment, a confirmation message and a premium receipt are sent to your registered email ID or mobile number. Online ULIP renewal is a secure and quick process , helping you maintain uninterrupted life cover and investment benefits.
Renewal through third-party applications
The customer can alternatively pay renewal premiums for their policies through third-party payment mobile applications like PayTM, Google Pay, PhonePe, and Amazon Pay, if these features are provided by their respective life insurance provider.
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP online
Before proceeding with your ULIP renewal online, take note of the following points to complete the payment securely and without delays:
Confirm the exact premium amount and the due date for your ULIP renewal.
Verify that your chosen payment method—whether it’s your bank account, debit card, or credit card—has sufficient funds or credit limit to cover the full premium amount.
Have your policy number ready to enter on the insurer’s website or mobile application.
Use the official online portal or the insurer’s dedicated mobile app to complete the transaction.
Verify that the payment page is secure by checking whether the URL starts with ‘https.’
Check your registered email address for the digital payment confirmation receipt, which confirms your successful ULIP renewal. 3 4
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP offline
If you’re renewing your policy offline, here are some things to keep in mind.
Make sure to write your policy number in addition to your name and phone number at the back of the cheque or demand draft if that’s your preferred mode of payment.
Call your insurer and/or your bank to ensure that the transaction has been successfully completed.
Key Takeaways
When you pay your ULIP renewal premium on time, your policy stays active.
Missing a renewal can cause your policy to lapse, which can result in interrupted life  insurance coverage and loss of tax benefits that you get under Section 80C (available under the old tax regime ).
You can renew your ULIP easily through multiple online or offline options, including auto-debit, UPI, or your insurer’s website or app. 5
Conclusion
It’s always advisable to consult with your insurer to get a better idea of the payment options they support. You can even contact your insurance agent who helped you source the policy. They will surely guide you in the same way. You can even go to the company website and check the customer support options and contact them for further instructions. Once you know the alternatives available to you, you can pick the mode that’s most convenient.
FAQs
Can I change my premium amount at the time of ULIP renewal?
You cannot change the regular premium amount. However, if your ULIP offers a Top-Up facility, you can invest an additional amount over your regular premium, subject to the terms and conditions of the policy and the insurers rules. 6
Is there a penalty for late ULIP renewal?
A ULIP policy cannot be renewed after a missed premium. If a premium is not paid within the grace period, the policy is treated as discontinued, and the only way to restore it is through policy revival, not renewal. Insurers typically allow a discontinued ULIP to be revived within the specified revival period, subject to certain conditions. . 7
Can I renew my ULIP policy after it has lapsed?
Once a ULIP has lapsed, it cannot be renewed. Insurers allow policyholders to revive a lapsed or discontinued ULIP within the specified revival period, subject to policy terms.
If the policy is revived within the allowed period, the insurance cover and policy benefits are restored. If it is not revived, the policy continues in discontinued status, and the fund value (if any) becomes payable only after completion of the applicable lock-in period.8
Is ULIP renewal mandatory every year?
ULIPs aren’t renewed annually. You must pay premiums as per your chosen frequency (monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annual) to maintain life cover, and tax benefits under Section 80C (only under the old tax regime).
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) Reg. Office Address: Bajaj Insurance House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959,  call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212 , mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. The Logo of Bajaj Life Insurance Limited is provided on the basis of license given by Bajaj Finserv Ltd. to use its “Bajaj” Logo.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy

Ways to Renew your ULIP policy
When you invest in a ULIP plan, you have the option to choose your premium payment mode. You can choose to pay your premium as a single lump sum or make periodic payments. Depending on the premium payment frequency, which can be monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, or annually, you are required to pay the premium monthly or according to the chosen frequency. Such payment is known as a renewal premium, since it contributes towards the renewal of your ULIP plan. Paying your premium on time is essential if you wish to continue enjoying your ULIP benefits. The Importance of Renewing Your ULIP Policy
Renewing your ULIP policy is a step that keeps your plan active. Whether you pay monthly, quarterly, half-
yearly or annually, each renewal maintains the continuity of your policy. Let’s understand why staying
consistent with ULIP renewal matters and how it impacts your financial planning. 1
● Ensures Continuation of ULIP Benefits
ULIPs offer you a protective life cover that comes in handy in the event of an
unfortunate incident. In addition to this, ULIPs also come with an investment component that
helps with capital appreciation. By renewing your ULIP policy, you can continue to enjoy these
benefits and maintain your financial safety net.
● ULIP renewal helps you keep your Life Goals on track
Failing to pay your renewal premium can result in the discontinuance of your policy.
That leaves you without an investment to rely on and an insurance cover to take refuge in.
● Continue to Enjoy ULIP Tax Benefits
Renewal not only ensures that you continue to reap the benefits of a ULIP, but it also
provides tax advantages. As per section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, premiums you pay
every year are deductible from your total income for that year. This reduces your taxable
income, and by consequence, your tax liability. ULIP tax benefits are subject to changes in tax
laws, if any.
What are the different ways to renew your ULIP?
You can pay the premium needed for ULIP renewal either online or offline. There are several options
available for making this payment, and depending on the payment options provided by the Insurer, you
can choose the option that’s most convenient for you. Here are some of the most common ways to
renew your ULIP.
● Renewal through cheque payment
This is the most conventional method for renewing ULIPs offline. You can either drop a
cheque for the amount of your renewal premium in a designated drop box at your life insurance
provider’s branch, or you could submit your cheque at the insurer’s office. Alternatively, you
could also submit the cheque at authorised bank partner collection centres.
● Renewal through ATM payments
This is another convenient mode of offline ULIP renewal. Many insurers are also
allowing policyholders to pay their renewal premiums through bank ATMs. If your insurer has a

your renewal payment.
● Renewal through your bank’s auto-debit facility
To ensure that you never miss another premium payment, you can opt for this mode of
ULIP renewal. You can activate the auto-debit facility either on your bank account or a Credit
card. Each time your premium is due, the amount will be automatically debited from your bank
account/credit card.
● Renewal through online payment
ULIP renewal can be completed easily through your insurer’s official website, customer portal, or mobile application. You can log in using your registered credentials, select your policy, click on the ‘Renew Policy’ or ‘Pay Premium’ option, and proceed to pay the renewal premium. 2
Most insurers accept multiple payment options, including
○ Debit and Credit Cards
○ Bank Transfers (NEFT, RTGS, or IMPS)
○ UPI
○ QR Payments
○ Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL)
○ Payment links
After successful payment, a confirmation message and a premium receipt are sent to your registered email ID or mobile number. Online ULIP renewal is a secure and quick process , helping you maintain uninterrupted life cover and investment benefits.
● Renewal through third-party applications
The customer can alternatively pay renewal premiums for their policies through third-
party payment mobile applications like PayTM, Google Pay, PhonePe, and Amazon Pay, if these
features are provided by their respective life insurance provider.
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP online
Before proceeding with your ULIP renewal online, take note of the following points to complete the
payment securely and without delays:
● Confirm the exact premium amount and the due date for your ULIP renewal.
● Verify that your chosen payment method—whether it’s your bank account, debit card, or
credit card—has sufficient funds or credit limit to cover the full premium amount.
● Have your policy number ready to enter on the insurer’s website or mobile application.
● Use the official online portal or the insurer’s dedicated mobile app to complete the
transaction.
● Verify that the payment page is secure by checking whether the URL starts with ‘https.’
● Check your registered email address for the digital payment confirmation receipt, which
confirms your successful ULIP renewal. 3 4
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP offline
If you’re renewing your policy offline, here are some things to keep in mind.
● Make sure to write your policy number in addition to your name and phone number at the
back of the cheque or demand draft if that’s your preferred mode of payment.
● Call your insurer and/or your bank to ensure that the transaction has been successfully
completed.
Key Takeaways
● When you pay your ULIP renewal premium on time, your policy stays active. ● Missing a renewal can cause your policy to lapse, which can result in interrupted life insurance coverage and loss of tax benefits that you get under Section 80C (available under the old tax regime ).
● You can renew your ULIP easily through multiple online or offline options, including auto- debit, UPI, or your insurer’s website or app. 5

It’s always advisable to consult with your insurer to get a better idea of the payment options they support.
You can even contact your insurance agent who helped you source the policy. They will surely guide you

contact them for further instructions. Once you know the alternatives available to you, you can pick the
mode that’s most convenient.
FAQs

You cannot change the regular premium amount. However, if your ULIP offers a Top-Up facility, you can
invest an additional amount over your regular premium, subject to the terms and conditions of the policy
and the insurers rules. 6

A ULIP policy cannot be renewed after a missed premium. If a premium is not paid within the grace period,

Once a ULIP has lapsed, it cannot be renewed. Insurers allow policyholders to revive a lapsed or

ULIPs aren’t renewed annually. You must pay premiums as per your chosen frequency (monthly, quarterly,

regime).

metadata.title: Revamp Ways to Renew your ULIP policy
